id: "18.1.13"
title: "Cisco ACI Bridge Domain Subnet Utilization (fvBD + fvSubnet, BD-Scoped IP-Pool Exhaustion, Gateway-Pool Depletion Forecasting, VM-Mobility Pressure)"
criticality: "high"

---

# UC-18.1.13 · Cisco ACI Bridge Domain Subnet Utilization (fvBD + fvSubnet, BD-Scoped IP-Pool Exhaustion, Gateway-Pool Depletion Forecasting, VM-Mobility Pressure)

## Description

Cisco Application Centric Infrastructure bridge domains (**fvBD**) anchor Layer-2 forwarding scopes while **fvSubnet** children declare pervasive gateway prefixes sized from compact management slices (/28) through broader east-west pools (/23). Each subnet contributes **total_ips** computed from **fvSubnet.ip** masks while live occupancy derives from **fvCEp** attachments grouped through **fvRsBd** lineage, distinct from contract counters, catalogue faults, or tenant audit trails because exhaustion gates gateway pools before workloads absorb mobility bursts. When **used_pct** climbs toward ninety-five percent, DHCP relays (**fvDhcpRelayP**) still broker discovers toward external pools yet attach failures multiply, so guest VMs stall during DHCP exchanges, orchestrators retry endlessly, and **scope=public** segments advertised via border-leaf **L3Out** risk losing granular EVPN specificity once gateway shelves empty.

## Value

Capacity planners gain runway telemetry grounded in APIC truth rather than spreadsheets; forecast stubs highlight dates where ninety-percent thresholds intersect procurement calendars while DHCP overlays reconcile **dhcp_lease_pool** reality versus fabric-visible counts. Splunk outputs steer subnet redesign budgets, DHCP relay remediation, coordinated CMDB freezes during migration bursts, and executive summaries tying **mobility_burst_window** frequency to VMware density forecasts so teams catch pool depletion before automation mid-wave.

## Implementation

Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**) for syslog parity, deploy a **300**-second modular poll that gathers **fvBD** children (**fvSubnet**, **fvDhcpRelayP**, **fvCEp** counts) into **`cisco:aci:bd_stats`**, compute **total_ips** and **used_pct**, and schedule capacity alerts at **85%**/**95%** with burst and expansion suppress macros.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Cisco APIC clusters running supported **6.0(x)**/**6.1(x)** trains reachable over HTTPS **443/tcp** from Splunk collectors; dedicate read-only REST identities isolated from GUI admins because overlapping **aaaRefresh.json** rotations collide across scripted collectors.
• Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**) wherever APIC syslog terminates so sibling sourcetypes such as **cisco:aci:syslog** keep mnemonic narratives (**F2378**, **F1296**) adjacent to REST-fed statistics without collapsing transports.
• Heavy Forwarder or Search Head tier hosts Python collectors built atop **python-aci-sdk** with **cobra** bindings (`pip install cobra`) aligned with Splunk modular-input packaging guidelines; stamp **collector_id** whenever multiple inputs authenticate toward one APIC VIP.
• Splunk roles executing capacity joins must include **index=cisco_aci** (rename per governance) plus **srchIndexesAllowed** entries covering scripted sourcetypes **cisco:aci:bd_stats** and **cisco:aci:endpoint**.
• Multicast BUM pressure stays an orthogonal metric; pair **arpFlood=yes**, **unkMacUcastAct=flood**, and dense **fvRsBd** attachments with companion dashboards instead of blending raw flood counters into **used_pct** denominators.

Step 1 ;  Configure data collection
Package Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (Splunkbase **4022**) then layer a Python modular input or scripted poll executed every **300** seconds:
• Authenticate via **POST** to **/api/aaaLogin.json** capturing **APIC-Cookie** plus refresh handles before issuing **GET** **/api/class/fvBD.json?query-target=children** so each **fvBD** Distinguished Name inherits **fvSubnet**, **fvDhcpRelayP**, and **fvRsBd** children inline.
• Emit newline-delimited JSON tagged **sourcetype=cisco:aci:bd_stats** carrying **tenant_dn**, **bd_name**, **bd_dn**, **fvSubnet.ip**, **fvSubnet.scope**, **fvSubnet.ctrl**, **arpFlood**, **multiDstPktAct**, **unkMacUcastAct**, and **total_ips** computed server-side as **pow(2,(32-mask_bits))-2** excluding reserved network plus broadcast corners.
• Poll **GET /api/class/fvSubnet.json** when collectors flatten subnets separately; each row records **scope** (private, shared, public) plus pervasive gateway semantics needed for **used_pct** denominators.
• Subscribe or poll **GET /api/class/fvCEp.json** at identical cadence (optionally **sourcetype=cisco:aci:endpoint**) counting **fvCEp** rows grouped by **fvRsBd** ancestry into **ip_in_use** per bridge domain.
• Optionally ingest **fvDhcpRelayP** relay pointers referencing external **dhcp_lease_pool** servers; Splunk enrichment macros later reconcile **dhcp_reserved_pct** gaps versus **fvCEp** counts.
• Expected volume: roughly one summarized row per **fvSubnet** per poll (~4 KB/event); multiply by subnet cardinality across tenants.
• Tag **dhcp_visibility_gap** fields (low, medium, high) inside collectors whenever engineers attach DHCP scopes wider than **fvBD** subnets.

Step 2 ;  Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Anchor alerting on **used_pct** thresholds (**critical ≥85%**, **urgent ≥95%**) plus **mobility_burst_window** flags derived from **streamstats** swings.

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:bd_stats" earliest=-8d@m latest=@m
| eval bd_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(bd_dn,fvBD_dn,fvbd_dn,distinguished_name,dn)))
| eval tenant_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_dn,fvtenant_dn,fvTenant_dn,tenant_hint)))
| eval vrf_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(vrf_dn,fvCtx_dn,fvctx_dn,fvCtx)))
| eval bd_name=trim(coalesce(bd_name,fvBD,name,fvbd_label))
| eval subnet_ip=trim(coalesce(subnet_ip,ip,fvsubnet_ip,fvSubnet))
| rex field=subnet_ip "(?<sip>[0-9.]+)/(?<cidr>[0-9]{1,2})(?:\s|$)"
| eval subnet_mask=tonumber(coalesce(subnet_mask,cidr_bits,prefix_len,cidr_mask,cidr))
| eval mb=case(isnotnull(subnet_mask),subnet_mask,isnotnull(cidr),tonumber(cidr),true(),null())
| eval mb=if(mb>=8 AND mb<=30,mb,null())
| eval total_ips=if(isnotnull(mb),pow(2,(32-mb))-2,null())
| eval ip_in_use=tonumber(coalesce(ip_in_use,fvcep_active,ep_attach_cnt,fvCEp_cnt,fvcep_cnt,fvcep_children,"0"))
| eval used_pct=if(total_ips>0 AND isnotnull(total_ips),round(100*ip_in_use/total_ips,3),null())
| eval subnet_scope=lower(trim(coalesce(subnet_scope,fvsubnet_scope,fvSubnet_scope,scope,"private")))
| eval dhcp_visibility_gap=lower(trim(coalesce(dhcp_visibility_gap,dhcp_gap_hint,"unknown")))
| bin _time span=6h as bin6
| stats avg(ip_in_use) as avg_ip avg(used_pct) as avg_pct max(used_pct) as peak_pct min(used_pct) as trough_pct latest(total_ips) as total_ips latest(subnet_scope) as subnet_scope latest(vrf_dn) as vrf_dn latest(bd_name) as bd_name latest(subnet_ip) as subnet_ip values(dhcp_visibility_gap) as dhcp_gap_vals by bd_dn tenant_dn vrf_dn subnet_ip bin6
| sort bd_dn subnet_ip bin6
| streamstats window=28 global=f current=f earliest(bin6) latest(bin6) avg(peak_pct) as roll_peak_pct values(subnet_scope) as scope_vals by bd_dn subnet_ip
| eval mobility_burst_window=if(peak_pct>=93 AND avg_pct>=87 AND peak_pct-roll_peak_pct>=10,"burst_candidate","steady_lane")
| eval subnet_expansion_signal=if(trough_pct>0 AND trough_pct<peak_pct-25 AND avg_pct<trough_pct+8,"possible_new_fvSubnet","stable_inventory")
| eval urgency=case(peak_pct>=95,"urgent_ge95",peak_pct>=85,"critical_ge85",peak_pct>=75,"elevated_ge75",1=1,"capacity_nominal")
| eval breach_lane=case(urgency IN ("urgent_ge95","critical_ge85"),urgency,mobility_burst_window=="burst_candidate","burst_pressure",subnet_expansion_signal=="possible_new_fvSubnet","inventory_refresh",1=1,"watch_only")
| where urgency!="capacity_nominal" OR mobility_burst_window=="burst_candidate" OR subnet_expansion_signal=="possible_new_fvSubnet"
| eval forecast_stub=if(isnotnull(avg_ip) AND isnotnull(total_ips),round((total_ips-avg_ip)/max(avg_ip*0.02,0.5),1),null())
| eval reconciler_hint=case(match(subnet_scope,"public"),"cross_check_border_leaf_exports_and_scope_public_leak_count",dhcp_visibility_gap=="high","merge_windows_dhcp_lease_pool_logs",true(),"audit_endpoint_aging_residual_timers")
| sort - peak_pct
| head 160
| table bin6 bd_dn tenant_dn vrf_dn bd_name subnet_ip subnet_scope total_ips avg_ip peak_pct trough_pct roll_peak_pct mobility_burst_window subnet_expansion_signal urgency breach_lane forecast_stub reconciler_hint dhcp_gap_vals
```

Parsing guidance: **bd_dn** MUST remain lowercase before **stats** merges; APIC distinguishes **uni/tn-*** casing inconsistently across SDK builds. **forecast_stub** approximates runway hours purely inside Splunk; swap with **predict** once **timechart** panels feed **algorithm=LLP** windows tied to **span=6h** grains.

Schedule searches every thirty minutes using cron expression */30 * * * * over **earliest=-8d@m** while paging **breach_lane** **urgent_ge95** immediately and **critical_ge85** within business-hour bridges unless **mobility_burst_window** suppress macros fire.

Step 3 ;  Validate
• APIC GUI path Tenants → Networking → Bridge Domains → choose BD → Operational → Client End-Points compares Splunk **ip_in_use** versus GUI totals within one polling interval.
• **moquery -c fvBD** from APIC CLI enumerates **fvSubnet** siblings exactly as collectors flatten JSON.
• **moquery -c fvSubnet** cross-checks **total_ips** math versus Splunk-evaluated **pow()** helpers.
• **scope=public** subnets demand extra confirmation against border-leaf **L3Out** route advertisements; ensure **scope_public_leak_count** logic stays consistent when **fvCEp** spans stretched forwarding domains.
• Capture **dhcp_lease_pool** counters from Microsoft DHCP or ISC logs; the delta versus **fvCEp** validates **dhcp_visibility_gap** tagging.

Step 4 ;  Operationalize
Dashboard rows recommended:
• Gauge tiles per **tenant_dn** highlighting worst **used_pct** bridge domains.
• Heat-map **bd_dn** versus **subnet_scope** mixing **critical_ge85** hues.
• Trend **predict** overlays over **avg_ip** curves forecasting ninety-percent breach dates.
• Leaderboard top-30 **bd_dn** pairs sorted by **mobility_burst_window** frequency capturing stretched fabrics.
• Companion panel correlating **fvDhcpRelayP** syslog (**F2378**) spikes while **dhcp_gap** climbs.

Step 5 ;  Tuning and extensions
• Correlate with UC-18.1.10 EPG health dashboards whenever **fvRsBd** bindings show healthy rolls yet **used_pct** alarms persist; signals subnet exhaustion masked as segment posture drift.
• Layer UC-18.1.3 endpoint mobility timelines tagging **mobility_burst_window** spikes versus planned-motion CMDB banners; suppress paging only when **burst_pressure** aligns with sanctioned migration-wave identifiers.
• Pipe DHCP authoritative logs via **lookup dhcp_scope_inventory** enriching **dhcp_reserved_pct** so **dhcp_visibility_gap** escalations cite numeric lease exhaustion rather than Splunk guesses alone.
• Extend **subnet_expansion_signal** watchers with Git **aci-export** commits referencing **fvSubnet** sibling inserts documenting **subnet_expansion_window** approvals.
• Tune **endpoint_aging_residual** alerts using **fvCEp** retention (900 seconds default) minus VMware vMotion stabilization timers recorded via **mobility_burst_window** overlays.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:bd_stats" earliest=-8d@m latest=@m
| eval bd_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(bd_dn,fvBD_dn,fvbd_dn,distinguished_name,dn)))
| eval tenant_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(tenant_dn,fvtenant_dn,fvTenant_dn,tenant_hint)))
| eval vrf_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(vrf_dn,fvCtx_dn,fvctx_dn,fvCtx)))
| eval bd_name=trim(coalesce(bd_name,fvBD,name,fvbd_label))
| eval subnet_ip=trim(coalesce(subnet_ip,ip,fvsubnet_ip,fvSubnet))
| rex field=subnet_ip "(?<sip>[0-9.]+)/(?<cidr>[0-9]{1,2})(?:\s|$)"
| eval subnet_mask=tonumber(coalesce(subnet_mask,cidr_bits,prefix_len,cidr_mask,cidr))
| eval mb=case(isnotnull(subnet_mask),subnet_mask,isnotnull(cidr),tonumber(cidr),true(),null())
| eval mb=if(mb>=8 AND mb<=30,mb,null())
| eval total_ips=if(isnotnull(mb),pow(2,(32-mb))-2,null())
| eval ip_in_use=tonumber(coalesce(ip_in_use,fvcep_active,ep_attach_cnt,fvCEp_cnt,fvcep_cnt,fvcep_children,"0"))
| eval used_pct=if(total_ips>0 AND isnotnull(total_ips),round(100*ip_in_use/total_ips,3),null())
| eval subnet_scope=lower(trim(coalesce(subnet_scope,fvsubnet_scope,fvSubnet_scope,scope,"private")))
| eval dhcp_visibility_gap=lower(trim(coalesce(dhcp_visibility_gap,dhcp_gap_hint,"unknown")))
| bin _time span=6h as bin6
| stats avg(ip_in_use) as avg_ip avg(used_pct) as avg_pct max(used_pct) as peak_pct min(used_pct) as trough_pct latest(total_ips) as total_ips latest(subnet_scope) as subnet_scope latest(vrf_dn) as vrf_dn latest(bd_name) as bd_name latest(subnet_ip) as subnet_ip values(dhcp_visibility_gap) as dhcp_gap_vals by bd_dn tenant_dn vrf_dn subnet_ip bin6
| sort bd_dn subnet_ip bin6
| streamstats window=28 global=f current=f earliest(bin6) latest(bin6) avg(peak_pct) as roll_peak_pct values(subnet_scope) as scope_vals by bd_dn subnet_ip
| eval mobility_burst_window=if(peak_pct>=93 AND avg_pct>=87 AND peak_pct-roll_peak_pct>=10,"burst_candidate","steady_lane")
| eval subnet_expansion_signal=if(trough_pct>0 AND trough_pct<peak_pct-25 AND avg_pct<trough_pct+8,"possible_new_fvSubnet","stable_inventory")
| eval urgency=case(peak_pct>=95,"urgent_ge95",peak_pct>=85,"critical_ge85",peak_pct>=75,"elevated_ge75",1=1,"capacity_nominal")
| eval breach_lane=case(urgency IN ("urgent_ge95","critical_ge85"),urgency,mobility_burst_window=="burst_candidate","burst_pressure",subnet_expansion_signal=="possible_new_fvSubnet","inventory_refresh",1=1,"watch_only")
| where urgency!="capacity_nominal" OR mobility_burst_window=="burst_candidate" OR subnet_expansion_signal=="possible_new_fvSubnet"
| eval forecast_stub=if(isnotnull(avg_ip) AND isnotnull(total_ips),round((total_ips-avg_ip)/max(avg_ip*0.02,0.5),1),null())
| eval reconciler_hint=case(match(subnet_scope,"public"),"cross_check_border_leaf_exports_and_scope_public_leak_count",dhcp_visibility_gap=="high","merge_windows_dhcp_lease_pool_logs",true(),"audit_endpoint_aging_residual_timers")
| sort - peak_pct
| head 160
| table bin6 bd_dn tenant_dn vrf_dn bd_name subnet_ip subnet_scope total_ips avg_ip peak_pct trough_pct roll_peak_pct mobility_burst_window subnet_expansion_signal urgency breach_lane forecast_stub reconciler_hint dhcp_gap_vals
```

## Visualization

(1) Radial gauge per **tenant_dn** showing worst **used_pct**. (2) Heat-map **bd_dn** × **subnet_scope** with critical banding at **85%**. (3) **predict** runway panel for ninety-percent crossing. (4) Leaderboard of stretched **bd_dn** rows sorted by **mobility_burst_window** frequency. (5) DHCP reconciliation tile contrasting **dhcp_lease_pool** versus **fvCEp** totals.
